# GC-02: End-to-End Traceability

**Track:** Governance Controls (`GC`) · **Priority:** MUST (mandatory) · **Status:** draft

Maintain bidirectional traceability linking every deployment artefact back to its originating intent, and every intent forward to its resulting deployment and monitoring state. Supports impact analysis for proposed changes.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 0 | Cross-Cutting | Mandatory |

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Fully automated |
| **Agent does** | Maintain the traceability graph continuously; expose query interface for impact analysis. |
| **Human does** | Invoke impact analysis queries; validate traceability completeness during audits. |

## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| Traceability graph | artefact | Bidirectional graph linking intent → design → code → tests → deployment → monitoring. | Lifecycle of the system + 7 years |
| Impact analysis report | report | On-demand report showing blast radius of a proposed or executed change. |  |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | continuous |
| **SLA** | — |
| **Tooling** | — |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** |  |
| **Escalate to** |  |



## Dependencies

None

## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 8(5) | Traceability of ICT changes through the full change management process |
| DORA | Art. 8(6) | Documented ICT risk management with full change history |
| EU AI Act | Art. 12(2) | Traceability of AI system functioning and decision-making |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Engineering Platform · **Owner role:** Platform Lead
